nav.switchWhenReady: Switch tabs after the current load finishes

containers.title: Containers
containers.execWindowMissingId: Missing container ID; cannot open the exec window.
containers.execHint: The main process runs docker exec -it … /bin/sh via node-pty; this pane is xterm.js (ANSI colors, vim, mysql password prompts, etc.). Type exit to leave the shell. Container must be running and include /bin/sh.
containers.hintNeedsRunning: Start the container first (state must be running).
containers.hintStartDisabled: Already running, paused, or removing — start is not available.
containers.hintStopDisabled: Only available while the container is running, restarting, or paused.
containers.hintKillPauseDisabled: Only available while the container is running or restarting.
containers.hintPauseDisabled: Pause is only available in the running state.
containers.hintUnpauseDisabled: Unpause is only available when the container is paused.
containers.execTerminalFootnote: Click the terminal or return to this window to focus; right-click the terminal area for a menu (copy, paste, copy all, clear output). Inside the pane you get a real shell (history, Tab). Ctrl+C goes to the process in the container.
containers.execTerminalCopyEmpty: Select text in the terminal with the mouse first, then use “Copy” from the right-click menu.
containers.execTerminalCopyAllEmpty: Nothing to copy from the buffer.
containers.execTerminalPasteDenied: Could not read the clipboard (permission denied).
containers.execTerminalTipClear: Clear the view only; keep the session
containers.execTerminalTipCopyAll: Select all buffer text and copy
containers.execTerminalTipPaste: Paste clipboard into the shell (bracketed paste)
containers.execImageHintComboAria: Suggested command: use ▼ to open the full template list, or edit the text, then insert
containers.execImageHintInsertTip: Paste the field contents into the shell (no Enter)
containers.execImageHintInsertRunTip: Paste into the shell and send Enter (use with care for multi-line or interactive commands)
containers.execImageHintPulseTitle: After paste: pick a command from the list, then Insert
containers.removeConfirm: Remove this container? Stopped containers can still be removed with force.
containers.contextConfigInPlaceHint: Rename and restart policy only; the container is not removed.
containers.contextConfigRecreateHint: Edit the full spec and recreate the container (image, ports, env, …).
containers.configRuntimeHint: Applied via Docker rename/update without removing the container. Auto-remove (--rm) containers only support restart policy "no". Memory/CPU/PIDs use engine update.
containers.exportTarConfirm: Export the container filesystem to a tar file at a path you choose. Continue?
containers.configHint: Saving stops and removes this container, then creates and starts a new one with your settings. Bind mounts and most host options are preserved from the original inspect data. Anonymous volume data may be lost — back up first.
containers.configRecreateConfirm: The current container will be removed (after stop) and recreated with the edited configuration. Continue?
containers.portsColumnHint: One mapping per line: left of " : " is the host publish side, right is container port/protocol. Host "—" means EXPOSE only, not published to the host.
containers.batchRemoveConfirm: Remove {{count}} selected container(s)? Running ones will be force-removed. Continue?
containers.colResizeHint: Drag to change this column width only; other columns stay the same.
containers.runtimeResourceHint: Memory/CPU/PIDs use engine update; changes may apply immediately or after a short delay on running containers.

images.title: Images
images.removeBulkConfirm: Remove {{count}} selected image(s)? This cannot be undone.
images.selectionHint: Click a row to select an image for tagging, history, and export; use checkboxes for multi-select delete.
images.emptyTable: No images yet. Pull an image above or load from a tar file.

networks.title: Networks
networks.removeBulkConfirm: Remove {{count}} selected network(s)? This cannot be undone.
networks.selectionHint: Click a row for connect actions below; use checkboxes for multi-select delete.
networks.emptyTable: No networks yet. Create one above.
networks.connectSelectAria: Container to attach to this network

volumes.title: Volumes
volumes.removeConfirm: Delete this volume? Data will be lost.
volumes.removeBulkConfirm: Delete {{count}} selected volume(s)? Data will be lost.
volumes.selectionHint: Click a row to see which containers use the volume; use checkboxes for multi-select delete.
volumes.emptyTable: No volumes yet. Create one above.

events.title: Docker events
events.subtitle: Engine event stream (JSON lines, summarized).
events.empty: Start subscription to see live events.

metrics.title: Resources
metrics.subtitle: CPU and memory for the computer running this app (Electron host). When the engine is connected, you also see engine totals, a sum of running containers’ RSS-style memory from docker stats, and image layer size if the System tab was opened once.
metrics.engineUnavailable: Engine not connected — host metrics still update. Connect Docker to load engine totals.
metrics.pausedHidden: Auto-refresh paused while this window is in the background.
metrics.engineContainersMemFootnote: Sum of each running container’s reported memory_stats.usage (docker stats, one-shot). Excludes stopped containers and engine/daemon overhead not attributed to a container.
metrics.engineContainersMemSkipped: {{n}} running container(s) omitted (stats missing or unreadable).
metrics.containersMemError: Could not load container memory summary: {{detail}}
metrics.meterDockerMem: Running containers memory vs engine total, about {{pct}} percent
metrics.engineDiskEmpty: Open the System tab once to load docker system df; then Layers size shows here from the app cache.
metrics.samplingNote: CPU usage is sampled over ~100 ms per request. Host + engine info load first; container memory is summed afterward so the page can render immediately. Many containers mean a longer second step—use a longer refresh interval if needed.

create.title: Create and start container
create.hint: Creates the container then starts it. Port format: host:container (e.g. 8080:80). CMD is split by spaces unless you leave it empty to use the image default.
create.name: Name
create.restartPolicyDisabledHint: Auto-remove cannot be combined with a restart policy; policy is set to “do not restart”.
create.dockerRunPlaceholder:
Type or paste a full command, e.g.:
docker run -d --name mysql -e MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=changeme -p 3306:3306 mysql:8.0
create.dockerRunFootnote: Runs docker on your machine like a terminal. Existing containers with the same name are not removed; rename or docker rm first if you get a name conflict.
create.dockerRunToolFilterPlaceholder: Type to fuzzy-match by id or title…
create.cliProgressDockerRun: Running docker run (may include image pull)…
create.cliProgressDockerfile: Running docker build, then starting the container…
create.dockerfilePlaceholder:
Edit your Dockerfile here…
(Build uses a temp directory with this file only; COPY . / context may fail—use a full project build in your terminal when needed.)
create.dockerfileFootnote: Runs docker build -t <tag> . in a temp folder, then docker run -d --name <derived> <tag>. Does not remove an existing container with that name.
create.dockerComposeProjectInvalid: Invalid project name: start with a letter or digit; only letters, digits, dots, underscores, hyphens.
create.dockerComposeFootnote: Writes this YAML as compose.yaml in a temp folder and runs docker compose up -d. Relative build paths only see that folder—full projects with COPY/build are best run from your repo in a terminal.

system.title: System
system.subtitle: Engine diagnostics and connection tools. App updates are under the Help menu.
system.jsonEmpty: No data yet. Ensure the engine is reachable, or use “Reset Docker client” below after changing DOCKER_HOST.
system.reconnectDockerHint: Recreates the internal client; restart the app after changing DOCKER_HOST etc.
system.swarmNote: Swarm / plugins: not managed here; use CLI or dedicated tools.
system.tlsNote: Remote TLS: configure DOCKER_HOST, DOCKER_TLS_VERIFY, DOCKER_CERT_PATH, then restart the app.
system.appUpdateMenuHint: Use Help → Check for App Updates / Restart to Install Update.
system.devBuildHint: Updates are only available in the installed release build (not dev).
system.updateNotAvailable: You are on the latest version.
system.updateDownloaded: Update downloaded. Restart to finish installation.

logs.title: Container logs
logs.hint: Right-click a row or the bottom details panel for the menu (exec, separate log window, and file manager).
logs.windowMissingId: Missing container id in the URL; cannot open the log window.
logs.listContainersFailed: Could not load containers to resolve the name: {{detail}}

files.title: Files
files.windowMissingId: The link is missing a container ID; cannot open the file manager window.
files.empty: This folder is empty or could not be listed.
files.treeTargetHint: Upload and new folder use: selected directory path; if a file is selected, its parent directory; if nothing is selected, the tree root.
files.hint: Tree with columns similar to ls -l: mode, link count, owner, group, size in bytes, mtime. Click ▸ or double-click folders to expand/collapse. Large downloads are tar; edit is UTF-8 whole-file. Deletes are permanent.
files.pickFileToEdit: Select a file (not a directory) to edit.
files.binaryNotEditable: This file is not valid UTF-8 text; download it instead.
files.copyPathFailed: Could not copy to the clipboard.
files.openFolderFailed: Could not resolve the save folder.
files.discardMkdirInput: A folder name was entered. Close without creating?
